items.title: Iran vows not a ‘single drop’ of oil or gas will pass strait of Hormuz as US carries out ninth night of strikes – Middle East crisis live
items.standfirst: Revolutionary Guard says it targeted US aircraft in Jordan and stopped two tankers in strait while American military says it is ‘holding Iran accountable’US launches ninth night of Iran strikes as strait of Hormuz standoff sees oil price pass $90 a barrelThe price of oil jumped in early Asian trade on Monday after the US carried out its latest wave of strikes on Iran and a vessel caught fire in the strait of Hormuz.Brent crude for September delivery rose 2.72% to $90.50 a barrel by around 0100 GMT, after briefly crossing $91 – its highest price since June.CENTCOM assets targeted Iranian military command centers, air defense and coastal surveillance sites, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and communications networks to further diminish Iran’s ability to attack commercial vessels and civilian mariners transiting the Strait of Hormuz.The U.S. military is holding Iran accountable at the Commander in Chief’s direction. Continue reading...
items.content: Revolutionary Guard says it targeted US aircraft in Jordan and stopped two tankers in strait while American military says it is ‘holding Iran accountable’US launches ninth night of Iran strikes as strait of Hormuz standoff sees oil price pass $90 a barrelThe price of oil jumped in early Asian trade on Monday after the US carried out its latest wave of strikes on Iran and a vessel caught fire in the strait of Hormuz.Brent crude for September delivery rose 2.72% to $90.50 a barrel by around 0100 GMT, after briefly crossing $91 – its highest price since June.CENTCOM assets targeted Iranian military command centers, air defense and coastal surveillance sites, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and communications networks to further diminish Iran’s ability to attack commercial vessels and civilian mariners transiting the Strait of Hormuz.The U.S. military is holding Iran accountable at the Commander in Chief’s direction. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: After the US military confirmed that a service member had been killed in Iraq – the third in recent days – Donald Trump said the new attacks were ‘in honour’ of the deadMiddle East crisis live: follow latest updatesThe US launched a ninth night of strikes targeting Iran early Monday, after announcing the death of another American service member, as the price of Brent crude oil climbed above $90 a barrel, the highest level since June.The US military said the service member was killed on Saturday in northern Iraq, where the US has military bases, during a controlled detonation of what was called unexploded ordnance on a downed Iranian attack drone. items.title: US announces death of another service member, Jordan summons Iranian envoy over attacks
items.standfirst: A US service member died during a "controlled detonation" of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone in northern Iraq, the military said Sunday. It followed the killing of two US troops in Jordan late Friday. Jordan on Sunday summoned Iran's top envoy in Amman to protest Tehran's attacks on its territory as Iran targeted US allies in the region in retaliation for US strikes across Iran.
items.content: A US service member died during a "controlled detonation" of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone in northern Iraq, the military said Sunday. It followed the killing of two US troops in Jordan late Friday. Jordan on Sunday summoned Iran's top envoy in Amman to protest Tehran's attacks on its territory as Iran targeted US allies in the region in retaliation for US strikes across Iran.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Spain crowned champions after extra-time thriller against Argentina
items.standfirst: Spain were crowned world champions for the second time in their history on Sunday, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. Ferran Torres' decisive strike in extra time settled a tightly contested final, giving Luis de la Fuente's Roja their second World Cup title, sixteen years after their first in 2010, and completing a historic Euro-World Cup double.
items.content: Spain were crowned world champions for the second time in their history on Sunday, defeating defending champions Argentina 1-0 after extra time at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. Ferran Torres' decisive strike in extra time settled a tightly contested final, giving Luis de la Fuente's Roja their second World Cup title, sixteen years after their first in 2010, and completing a historic Euro-World Cup double.

items.title: Spain-Argentina: La Roja claim second World Cup title in extra time
items.standfirst: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey to win the World Cup, completing a historic Euro-World Cup double. Ferran Torres' late strike sealed victory for a dominant Spanish side against a 10-man Argentina, ending Lionel Messi's World Cup career on a bittersweet note. Relive our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.
items.content: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey to win the World Cup, completing a historic Euro-World Cup double. Ferran Torres' late strike sealed victory for a dominant Spanish side against a 10-man Argentina, ending Lionel Messi's World Cup career on a bittersweet note. Relive our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.

Reported by CBS News, NPR News, NBC World

items.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
items.standfirst: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.
items.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

items.title: Latest details on arrests of Andrew and Tristan Tate
items.standfirst: U.S. Marshals arrested social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami on Saturday due to recent developments in the U.K. Authorities there, who already had a case against the duo, say four more alleged victims of the brothers came forward with accusations including rape, sex trafficking, assault and child pornography. Cristian Benavides reports.
items.content: U.S. Marshals arrested social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami on Saturday due to recent developments in the U.K. Authorities there, who already had a case against the duo, say four more alleged victims of the brothers came forward with accusations including rape, sex trafficking, assault and child pornography. Cristian Benavides reports.

clusters.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
bullet_summary: Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles
Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
items.standfirst: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.
items.content: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv for five hours in one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks
items.standfirst: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injuredRussia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital.Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. Residents heard an air raid siren at 1.30am. There was the sound of air defences, followed minutes later by a series of booms and explosions. Continue reading...
items.content: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injuredRussia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital.Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. Residents heard an air raid siren at 1.30am. There was the sound of air defences, followed minutes later by a series of booms and explosions. Continue reading...

clusters.title: ICE officers to use body cameras during vehicle stops, Trump ‘border czar’ says
clusters.neutral_headline: ICE officers to use body cameras during vehicle stops, Trump ‘border czar’ says
bullet_summary: White House border czar Tom Homan said "I think body cameras are the way to go" after fatal shootings by federal immigration officers in Texas and Maine earlier this month
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: ICE officers to use body cameras during vehicle stops, Trump ‘border czar’ says
items.standfirst: Tom Homan’s comments come amid scrutiny over federal immigration agents’ fatal shooting of two men in six daysUS Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will require its officers to record vehicle stops with at least one body camera, said Tom Homan, the Trump administration “border czar”, on Sunday. The action comes as the agency is under renewed scrutiny after ICE officers fatally shot two men earlier this month.“They exonerate more law enforcement than they convict, and I want officers to wear body cameras because I want the American people to see what the ⁠officers saw when they took that action,” Homan said on the Fox & Friends Weekend program. Continue reading...
items.content: Tom Homan’s comments come amid scrutiny over federal immigration agents’ fatal shooting of two men in six daysUS Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) will require its officers to record vehicle stops with at least one body camera, said Tom Homan, the Trump administration “border czar”, on Sunday. The action comes as the agency is under renewed scrutiny after ICE officers fatally shot two men earlier this month.“They exonerate more law enforcement than they convict, and I want officers to wear body cameras because I want the American people to see what the ⁠officers saw when they took that action,” Homan said on the Fox & Friends Weekend program. Continue reading...

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.standfirst: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
items.content: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.(Image credit: David Santiago)
